data.title: Banner GAAP EPS of $1.30 beats by $0.08, revenue of $160.2M beats by $0.53M
data.description:
• Banner press release (NASDAQ:BANR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.30 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $160.2M (+10.8% Y/Y) beats by $0.53M.
    • Net interest income was $141.1 million in the first quarter of 2025, compared to $140.5 million in the preceding quarter and increased 6% from $133.0 million in the first quarter a year ago.
    • Mortgage banking operations revenue was $3.1 million for the first quarter of 2025, compared to $3.7 million in the preceding quarter and $2.3 million in the first quarter a year ago.
    • Return on average assets was 1.15% for both the current and preceding quarter and 0.97% in the first quarter a year ago.
    • Net loans receivable increased to $11.28 billion at March 31, 2025, compared to $11.20 billion at December 31, 2024, and increased 5% compared to $10.72 billion at March 31, 2024.
    • Non-performing assets were $42.7 million, or 0.26% of total assets, at March 31, 2025, compared to $39.6 million, or 0.24% of total assets, at December 31, 2024 and $29.9 million, or 0.19% of total assets, at March 31, 2024.
    • The allowance for credit losses - loans was $157.3 million, or 1.38% of total loans receivable, as of March 31, 2025, compared to $155.5 million, or 1.37% of total loans receivable, as of December 31, 2024 and $151.1 million, or 1.39% of total loans receivable, as of March 31, 2024.
    • Total deposits increased to $13.59 billion at March 31, 2025, compared to $13.51 billion at December 31, 2024, and increased 3% compared to $13.16 billion at March 31, 2024.
    • Core deposits represented 89% of total deposits at March 31, 2025.
    • Dividends paid to shareholders were $0.48 per share in the quarter ended March 31, 2025.
    • Common shareholders’ equity per share increased 3% to $53.16 at March 31, 2025, compared to $51.49 at the preceding quarter end, and increased 10% from $48.39 at March 31, 2024.
    • Tangible common shareholders’ equity per share* increased 4% to $42.27 at March 31, 2025, compared to $40.57 at the preceding quarter end, and increased 13% from $37.40 at March 31, 2024.

    *Non-GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) financial measure; See, “Additional Financial Information - Non-GAAP Financial Measures” on the final two pages of this press release for a reconciliation of non-GAAP financial measures.

    Income Statement Review

data.title: CVR Energy nearly completes maintenance at Coffeyville refinery - report
data.description:
CVR Energy's (NYSE:CVI) Coffeyville refinery in Kansas should soon complete planned turnaround work, Dow Jones reported late Tuesday, adding that the plant may have been working through some issues during the restart process.

The report said a source said late last week that operators were planning to bring the 136K bbl/day refinery back online by this Friday - although this could be delayed by restart issues - but another source said CVR Energy (NYSE:CVI) was selling Group 3 ULSD (X-grade), which could mean the refinery's distillation unit has returned to service.

CEO Dave Lamp said in CVR Energy's (CVI) Q4 earnings call that the company would extend maintenance work at the Coffeyville refinery by 10-15 days, and the turnaround was expected to be completed by end of March, following a January fire at the plant's naphtha hydrotreater.

CVR (CVI) also said it was planning to use the turnaround to finish tie-in work for the initial phase of a project to increase the refinery's distillate production by 1,500 bbl/day.

data.title: Dollar Tree CFO buys shares worth ~$1.24M
data.description:
Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) said in a filing on Wednesday that CFO Stewart Glendinning has acquired shares worth ~$1.24M in the retail discount stores company.

DLTR shares were +2.09% to $73.30 ahead of the closing bell.

Glendinning bought 4,000 shares of common stock for $72.5 per share, and 13,000 shares for a weighted average price per share of $72.8269.

The CFO owns 49,353 shares of direct ownership post the transaction.

data.title: OCC blends large, midsize and community bank oversight in organizational overhaul
data.description:
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency is merging its supervision of large, midsize and community banks, aiming to bolster efficiency in regulatory oversight, it said in a statement released Wednesday.

Organizational changes will take effect starting June 2, 2025.

The combination of the two supervision functions will effectively create the Bank Supervision and Examination line of business, enabling the OCC to smoothly share expertise and resources to address bank-specific issues, it said.

Greg Coleman, senior deputy comptroller for Large Bank Supervision, will lead the Bank Supervision and Examination office. 

In addition, the Chief National Bank Examiner, to be led by Jay Gallagher, the senior deputy comptroller for Supervision Risk and Analysis office will be reinstated. That will include the divisions of Bank Supervision Policy as well as Supervision Risk and Analysis.

The banking regulator also noted that it is elevating its Information Technology and Security function to be led by a new senior deputy comptroller for ITS, who will serve as a member of the Executive Committee.

data.title: New US export requirements on Nvidia, AMD part of effort against China's AI ambitions: report
data.description:
The new export licensing requirement targeting Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) artificial intelligence chips are part of a larger effort to block China's AI ambitions, according to a report by The New York Times.

Nvidia and AMD shares had both tumbled nearly 10% by afternoon trading on Wednesday.

The Trump Administration is considering applying penalties to block DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK), a China-based AI company, from purchasing any U.S. technology, the report said. It is also considering blocking Americans' access to DeepSeek. 

What's more, the House Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party, released a new investigative report today that finds DeepSeek operates on tens of thousands of Nvidia chips, including some that are subject to U.S. export controls.

"This report makes it clear: DeepSeek isn't just another AI app — it's a weapon in the Chinese Communist Party's arsenal, designed to spy on Americans, steal our technology, and subvert U.S. law," said Chairman John Moolenaar (R-MI). "We now know this tool exploited U.S. AI models and reportedly used advanced Nvidia chips that should never have ended up in CCP hands. That's why we're sending a letter to Nvidia to demand answers. American innovation should never be the engine of our adversaries' ambitions."

Nvidia said it follows the proper protocol laid out by the U.S. government regarding the export of its hardware.

"The U.S. government instructs American businesses on what they can sell and where—we follow the government’s directions to the letter," a Nvidia spokesperson told Seeking Alpha.  "NVIDIA protects and enhances national security by creating U.S. jobs and infrastructure, promoting U.S. technology leadership, bringing billions of dollars of tax revenue to the U.S. treasury, and alleviating the massive U.S. trade deficit."

"The technology industry supports America when it exports to well-known companies worldwide - if the government felt otherwise, it would instruct us. Our reported Singapore revenue indicates the billing address, often for subsidiaries of our U.S. customers," the spokesperson added. "The associated products are shipped to other locations, including the United States and Taiwan, not to China."

data.title: Wipro targets $2.505B-$2.557B in Q1 FY26 IT services revenue amid macro challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Wipro Limited (WIT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Srinivas Pallia highlighted that Wipro secured two mega deals this year, reflecting strong growth in large deal bookings. For FY25, the company closed 63 large deals amounting to $5.4 billion, a year-on-year growth of 17.5%. He emphasized the importance of AI-powered solutions, noting that AI has become central to almost every opportunity, driving productivity and efficiency for clients.
  • Pallia discussed the performance of strategic market units, reporting growth in Americas 1 but declines in Europe and APMEA. He also noted a 13.4% sequential increase in Q4 order bookings to $4.0 billion.
  • CFO Aparna Iyer stated that operating margins for Q4 remained steady at 17.5%, a year-on-year increase of 1.1%. Full-year operating margins expanded by 0.9% to 17.1%. Net income increased by 6% quarter-on-quarter in Q4 and by 19% for the full year.
  • Iyer mentioned that free cash flow as a percentage of net income stood at 118% for FY25, with gross cash including investments totaling $6.4 billion.

Outlook

  • Management provided guidance for Q1 FY26, with IT services revenue expected to range from $2.505 billion to $2.557 billion, representing a sequential decline of 3.5% to 1.5% in constant currency.
  • CEO Pallia acknowledged that clients are adopting a measured approach to large transformation programs and discretionary spending due to macroeconomic uncertainties, including tariff impacts.

Financial Results

  • IT services revenue for Q4 FY25 was $2.6 billion, reflecting a sequential decline of 0.8% and a year-on-year decrease of 1.2%. Full-year IT services revenues declined by 2.3% year-on-year to $10.51 billion.
  • Operating margins for Q4 were 17.5%, consistent with the previous quarter but up 110 basis points year-on-year.
  • Strategic market unit performance showed mixed results, with Americas 1 growing 0.2% sequentially, while Europe and APMEA experienced declines. Capco posted strong growth with a 6.5% sequential increase and an 11.5% year-on-year rise.

Q&A

  • Nitin Padmanabhan, Investec, inquired about sectoral impacts and client approaches. Pallia responded that sectors like consumer and manufacturing are experiencing direct impacts, while BFSI shows strong pipeline momentum but cautious client spending.
  • Vibhor Singhal, Nuvama Equities, asked about revenue trends for FY26. Pallia indicated that macroeconomic uncertainties and tariff scenarios are key factors influencing revenue growth, with some large transformation programs paused or delayed.
  • Gaurav Rateria, Morgan Stanley, queried about large deal conversions and deal tenors. CFO Iyer noted that while large deals are robust, medium-sized deals have picked up, improving deal conversion metrics.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed skepticism about the company’s ability to navigate macroeconomic challenges effectively, particularly regarding client cautiousness in discretionary spending. Questions often sought clarity on specific sectoral impacts and guidance assumptions.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, highlighting strong deal momentum and operational improvements. However, acknowledgment of uncertainties and client conservatism reflected a measured stance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more defensive, particularly when addressing the challenging macroeconomic conditions and their influence on revenue guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for Q1 FY26 indicated a sequential decline compared to Q4 FY25, reflecting a more cautious outlook amid macroeconomic uncertainties.
  • Strategic focus remained consistent, with continued emphasis on large deals and AI-powered solutions. However, the tone shifted to address more pressing concerns over discretionary spending and transformation program delays.
  • Analysts maintained a critical stance, probing deeper into the specifics of guidance assumptions and sectoral dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified macroeconomic uncertainties, including tariff impacts, as a significant headwind influencing client decision-making and revenue growth.
  • Analysts raised concerns over the duration of cautious client spending and the potential impact on large deal conversions and revenue growth.
  • Management cited ongoing efforts to address these challenges through focused deal closures and client-centric strategies, including leveraging AI for cost efficiencies and operational improvements.

Final Takeaway

Wipro Limited reported a steady performance for Q4 FY25 with stable operating margins and strong large deal momentum, closing 63 deals worth $5.4 billion for the year. However, revenue guidance for Q1 FY26 indicates a cautious outlook, reflecting macroeconomic uncertainties and client conservatism. Management remains focused on leveraging AI-driven solutions and expanding strategic market units to mitigate external challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nasdaq-100 crumbles as tech sells off and Powell's remarks fan the flames
data.description:
The Nasdaq-100 index (NDX) tumbled 4.1% on Wednesday, weighed down by a rout in technology stocks and hawkish commentary from Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) disclosed it had received a notice from the U.S. government requiring a special license to export its H20 graphics processing units and related hardware to China. The world's third-largest company by market cap also warned of a $5.5B hit to its quarterly results from the export controls.

Meanwhile, NVDA rival AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) also cautioned of a $800M hit from export controls to China. 

Additionally, Dutch chip giant ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) missed quarterly bookings expectations and said tariffs were increasing uncertainty for the long term. 

The negative updates from the semiconductor behemoths weighed on the chip space. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) - a key barometer for the industry - slid 6.9%.  

Sentiment took a further beating in the afternoon following remarks from Fed chief Powell. He highlighted a deceleration in U.S. economic growth during the first quarter and reiterated his expectation that the impact of tariffs may prove more severe than initially anticipated.

The sell-off in the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) was broad-based, with 99 out of 100 constituent stocks in the red, underscoring the depth of the market’s retreat. 

Among the hardest hit in the NDX was Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), which saw its stock plummet by 9.9%, leading the day’s declines.

Below are the 10 worst-performing stocks in the Nasdaq-100 as of 3:15 pm EST. 

No. 10: Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) -7.4%.

No. 9: Tesla (TSLA) -7.5%.

No. 8: On Semiconductor Corporation (NASDAQ:ON) -7.8%.

No. 7: GlobalFoundaries (NASDAQ:GFS) -7.8%.

No. 6: KLA Corporation (NASDAQ:KLAC) -7.9%.

No. 5: Applovin (NASDAQ:APP) -8.1%.

No. 4: ASML Holdings (NASDAQ:ASML) -8.2%.

No. 3: Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) -8.7%.

No. 2: Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) -9.8%.

No. 1: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) -9.9%.

The broader market weakness also dragged down the Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) —a major ETF tracking the Nasdaq-100. With nearly $300B in assets under management, QQQ fell 4.1% on the day.

Other ETFs with significant exposure to the Nasdaq-100 or the broader Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) also are in the market’s crosshairs: (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NASDAQ:SQQQ), and (NYSEARCA:QID).
